summary: NEWCASTLE UNITED FOOTBALL COMPANY LIMITED (LEI 213800AG2V6YE68H5N63, GB-COH 00031014) is identifier-confirmed and registered in the United Kingdom. UK Companies House records show that Newcastle United Limited holds 75% or more of shares in the company, while GLEIF Level 2 data identifies Public Investment Fund as the ultimate parent (accounting consolidation), a relationship also corroborated by OpenSanctions; however, GLEIF additionally records a reporting exception indicating that an intermediate parent exists but holds no LEI, introducing opacity into the ownership chain. The board comprises six directors per UK Companies House and OpenCorporates: James Adam Reuben (from 2021-10-07), Yasir Othman Alrumayyan (from 2021-12-02), Abdulmajid Ahmed Alhagbani (from 2023-02-23), Asmaa Mohammed Rezeeq (from 2023-02-23), Roger Thornton (from 2024-07-25), and Jacobo Francisco Solis Patino (from 2024-12-16). Two structural risk signals were identified: an opaque ownership signal (medium confidence, per GLEIF) arising from an anonymous entity in the ownership chain, and a non-EU/EEA jurisdiction signal (high confidence, per UK Companies House) reflecting that the ownership chain reaches into GB, a condition relevant under AMLA CDD RTS criterion (b). A material limitation applies: OpenSanctions data is licensed under CC-BY-NC-4.0 and may not be commercially re-used.
